description: Implement linear regression on sklearn.datasets.load_diabetes with L1 (Lasso) regularization using PyTorch. Use AdamW optimizer; manual L1 penalty or weight decay. Train/validation split; self-verifying exit code.

requirements.math: Docstring must define Lasso objective: MSE + $\lambda \|\theta\|_1$ (exclude bias from penalty).
requirements.data: Use sklearn.datasets.load_diabetes; 80% train / 20% validation; StandardScaler (manual or sklearn).
requirements.implementation: torch.nn.Linear; AdamW; add L1 penalty on weights in loss (no autograd for L1 subgradient).
requirements.evaluation: evaluate() returns MSE, R2 on validation; main asserts R2 > 0.35 and MSE < 3500.
requirements.main_block: Train, evaluate on train and val, print metrics, assert thresholds, sys.exit(0) on success else sys.exit(1).

description: Implement multivariate linear regression using torch.optim.LBFGS (full-batch). Compare convergence vs SGD or Adam on the same data.

requirements.math: Docstring: state that L-BFGS is a quasi-Newton method approximating the Hessian.
requirements.data: Synthetic: X [200, 8], y = X @ beta + noise; 80/20 train/val split.
requirements.implementation: nn.Linear; closure-based LBFGS step; optional comparison with SGD (log epochs to convergence).
requirements.evaluation: evaluate() returns MSE, R2; main asserts R2 > 0.85 on validation.
requirements.main_block: Train with LBFGS, evaluate, print metrics, assert quality, sys.exit(0) or sys.exit(1).

description: Multiclass logistic regression (softmax) on sklearn.datasets.load_wine. L2 regularization, StandardScaler, macro-F1 and accuracy.

requirements.math: Docstring: softmax and cross-entropy in LaTeX; mention L2 regularization on weights.
requirements.implementation: nn.Linear(in, 3); CrossEntropyLoss; Adam or SGD with weight_decay.
requirements.evaluation: evaluate() returns accuracy, macro-F1, per-class metrics on validation.
requirements.main_block: Train, evaluate train/val, print metrics, assert accuracy > 0.85 and macro-F1 > 0.80, sys.exit(0/1).

algorithm: Logistic Regression (LR Scheduler + L1 Feature Selection)
description: Binary logistic regression on a real dataset (e.g. sklearn breast_cancer or digits binarized) with a learning-rate scheduler (StepLR or OneCycleLR) and optional L1 penalty for feature selection. Report top-k selected features.

requirements.data: sklearn.datasets.load_breast_cancer (or load_digits with binary target); train/val split; StandardScaler.
requirements.implementation: nn.Linear; Adam; torch.optim.lr_scheduler.StepLR or OneCycleLR; add L1 penalty on weights to loss.
requirements.evaluation: evaluate() returns accuracy, F1, and list of top-5 feature indices by |weight|.
requirements.main_block: Train with scheduler, evaluate, print metrics and top features, assert accuracy > 0.92, sys.exit(0) or sys.exit(1).
